input.background: The time to failure of a component follows a gamma distribution with parameters \( \alpha = 3 \) and \( \beta = 5 \). The problem involves determining the reliability of the component and the hazard rate at 10 time-units, as well as understanding the properties of the gamma distribution.
input.question: Provide the mean, variance, and reliability of the gamma random variable.

output.answer: The mean, variance, and reliability of the gamma random variable are, respectively: $$ \begin{gathered} \text{Mean (MTTF)} \quad E(T)=\frac{\alpha}{\beta} \\ \text{Variance} \quad V(T)=\frac{\alpha}{\beta^2} \\ \text{Reliability} \quad R(t)=\int_t^{\infty} \frac{\beta^\alpha x^{\alpha-1}}{\Gamma(\alpha)} e^{-x \beta} d x \end{gathered} $$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
